classObjective: Calculate the rate of change from tables, graphs, and real-world data and compute the slope of a line passing through two given points.
workedExamples: 4-2, Example 1 — Find the Rate of Change from a Table
4-2, Example 2 — Find the Rate of Change from a Graph
warmUp: If you drive 150 miles in 3 hours, what is your average speed?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: rate of change formula (rise over run); interpreting rates in context; slope from tables and graphs
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice calculating rate of change from tables and graphs and computing slope from two points
independentPractice: Practice problems finding rate of change and slope in various contexts
assessment: Exit ticket: find the slope of the line passing through (2, 5) and (6, 13)
capReflection: How is rate of change related to the slope of a line?
